preview_text:
The model favors Portugal at 77%, with DR Congo positioned as a substantial underdog at 3.5%. The gap between favorite and second-place outcome is substantial, reflecting a clear expected hierarchy in this fixture.

The win–draw–loss spread reveals a dominant-favorite scenario. Portugal's win probability exceeds the combined likelihood of a draw and DR Congo victory by a wide margin. A draw remains plausible at roughly one in five matches, but the modal outcome is a Portugal victory by a meaningful margin. Competitiveness on paper appears limited by these figures.

The model and sportsbook consensus align closely across all three outcomes, with differences under 5 percentage points. Polymarket prices similarly cluster within that band. The slight variance—sportsbooks assign DR Congo marginally higher odds than the model—does not signal material divergence in assessment. No systematic disagreement between sources is evident.
